title: Electrical and Electronics Installers and Repairers, Transportation Equipment
description: Install, adjust, or maintain mobile electronics communication equipment, including sound, sonar, security, navigation, and surveillance systems on trains, watercraft, or other mobile equipment.

tasks.text: Inspect and test electrical systems and equipment to locate and diagnose malfunctions, using visual inspections, testing devices, and computer software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Reassemble and test equipment after repairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust, repair, or replace defective wiring and relays in ignition, lighting, air-conditioning, and safety control systems, using electrician's tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Splice wires with knives or cutting pliers, and solder connections to fixtures, outlets, and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Locate and remove or repair circuit defects such as blown fuses or malfunctioning transistors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Refer to schematics and manufacturers' specifications that show connections and provide instructions on how to locate problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install fixtures, outlets, terminal boards, switches, and wall boxes, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install new fuses, electrical cables, or power sources as required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut openings and drill holes for fixtures, outlet boxes, and fuse holders, using electric drills and routers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with customers to determine the nature of malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install electrical equipment such as air-conditioning, heating, or ignition systems and components such as generator brushes and commutators, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair or rebuild equipment such as starters, generators, distributors, or door controls, using electrician's tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate costs of repairs based on parts and labor requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure, cut, and install frameworks and conduit to support and connect wiring, control panels, and junction boxes, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
